items.headline: Bryson Graham is ready to take on the challenge of rebuilding the struggling Bulls
items.description:
CHICAGO (AP) — The tears flowed as soon as Bryson Graham got the call from the Chicago Bulls. Once they stopped, he turned his attention to the task at hand.
Graham has a big job trying to lift a struggling franchise after Chicago hired him as executive vice president of basketball operations on Monday. Though he's from San Antonio and rooted for the Spurs, he was also a fan of the Bulls growing up in the 1990s. He understands what the team means to the city and the NBA, decades after Michael Jordan and Scottie Pippen captured their sixth championship.

items.headline: Daniss Jenkins goes from being an undrafted free agent on a 2-way contract to key player for Pistons
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Daniss Jenkins was overlooked and underappreciated for much of his career.
Not anymore.

items.headline: Luka Doncic says being injured during Lakers' playoff run has been 'very frustrating'
items.description:
OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Luka Doncic said Wednesday he was given platelet-rich plasma injections in Spain in an effort to speed up his eight-week timeline for return from the hamstring injury that has had him sidelined since April 2.
“I went to Spain to do PRP,” Doncic told reporters. “Everybody knows that its one of the best countries to do that. Obviously, you know, we talked with the Lakers doctors, so everybody agreed for me to go there.”

items.headline: Cavaliers guard Sam Merrill gets MRI on hamstring and sits out practice after Game 1 injury
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Cleveland Cavaliers reserve guard Sam Merrill had an MRI on his left hamstring after injuring it in a Game 1 loss to the Detroit Pistons.
The Cavs held him out of practice Wednesday, a day before they will shoot to even the second-round series in Detroit.

items.headline: Celtics' Brad Stevens still reconciling how season that had sudden title hopes ended so quickly
items.description:
BOSTON (AP) — Make no mistake about it, Brad Stevens still expected to be watching the Celtics in the playoffs right now.
“I’m pissed. I’d rather be playing New York tonight,” Boston’s president of basketball operations said Wednesday during his end of season news conference.

items.headline: Joel Embiid is ruled out with ankle, hip injuries for the 76ers in Game 2 of their playoff series against the Knicks
items.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Joel Embiid is ruled out with ankle, hip injuries for the 76ers in Game 2 of their playoff series against the Knicks.

items.headline: Joel Embiid is out for Game 2 of the 76ers' series against the Knicks with ankle and hip injuries
items.description:
NEW YORK (AP) — Joel Embiid was sidelined for Game 2 of the Philadelphia 76ers' second-round series against the New York Knicks on Wednesday night with a sprained right ankle and a sore right hip.
The 76ers had listed their center as probable to play, mentioning only the ankle, before adding the hip problem on the injury report and ruling him out about six hours before trying to even the Eastern Conference semifinals.

items.headline: Jalen Duren delivers for the Pistons, making plays at both ends of court in key moments against Cavs
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Jalen Duren delivered for the Detroit Pistons when they needed him most, making plays at both ends of the court against the Cleveland Cavaliers.
Duren had three dunks, two rebounds and a block in a 67-second stretch to break open a tie game and Detroit went on to beat Cleveland 111-101 on Tuesday night in Game 1 of their second-round series.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham scores 23, Tobias Harris has 20 to help Pistons beat Cavs in Game 1
items.description:
DETROIT (AP) — Cade Cunningham scored 23 points, Tobias Harris had 20 and the Detroit Pistons beat the Cleveland Cavaliers 111-101 on Tuesday night in Game 1 of their second-round series.
Duncan Robinson added 19 points for the top-seeded Pistons, who ended an NBA record-tying 12-game postseason losing streak against a single opponent, a drought that dated to the 2007 Eastern Conference finals.

items.headline: Lakers forward Vanderbilt suffers 'gruesome' injury to right pinkie while trying to block shot
items.description:
OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Los Angeles Lakers forward Jarred Vanderbilt injured his right pinkie when he attempted to block a dunk by Oklahoma City Thunder center Chet Holmgren during Game 1 of their Western Conference semifinal matchup on Tuesday night.
Several players on the Thunder bench immediately looked away at the sight of the finger.
